---
title: "Research"
---

## Working papers

### An Industry-Level Measure of Quality-Adjusted Human Capital in the European Union

*With [Juan Durán Vanegas](https://www.esri.ie/people/juan-duran-vanegas) and [Iulia Siedschlag](https://www.esri.ie/people/iulia-siedschlag). ESRI Working Paper No. 812, October 2025.*

[[Paper]](#){.paper-btn} [[Policy brief]](#){.paper-btn}

This paper examines the role of human capital in driving labour-productivity growth across industries in the European Union and the United Kingdom from 2010 to 2021. Building on Égert et al. (2022), we introduce an industry-level quality-adjusted human capital (QAHC) measure that combines the quantity of education with its quality, drawing on test-score data from the OECD Programme for International Student Assessment (PISA) and Programme for the International Assessment of Adult Competencies (PIAAC), mean years of schooling, and industry employment composition from EU KLEMS and INTANProd. Estimated within a Stochastic Frontier framework, the measure yields plausible output elasticities and is statistically associated with labour-productivity growth, particularly in goods-producing industries. These results stand in contrast to those obtained using conventional education-attainment proxies, which fail to capture a comparable productivity link.

---

## Theses

### The Impact of Light Rail Transit Extensions on Residential Property Prices: Evidence from Dublin's Luas Cross City Project

*Master's thesis, Sciences Po Paris, 2025.*

[[Thesis]](#){.paper-btn}
